Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

BagM Beih. 02, 010 [Temple-building rituals][via cams/gkab]

Top
t.e. 1t.e. 1

[... an]-⸢tu₄? liš?-lim?⸣

(t.e. 1) May it go well [...] Antu.

Obverse
o 1o 1

[...] x x [x x] ⸢uš⸣-šu-ši ⸢ša₂ E₂⸣ [...]

(o 1) [...] renovating of [...] temple, [...] a favourable month on a [...] during the night [...] 3 offering tables for the god [...], the [...] of the temple and the protective spirit [...]. You lay out an offering. You present shoulder, fatty tissue and roast meat. You libate first-class beer, wine (and) [...]. You set fire to a pile of brushwood for Ea and Asalluhi.

o 22

[x] ITI šal-mu ⸢ina⸣ [...] ina GE₆ 3 ⸢GI⸣.[DU₈]-⸢MEŠ⸣ a-⸢na DINGIR⸣ [x]

o 3-43-4

[...] E₂ u d[LAMMA ...] ⸢uduSISKUR₂ BAL⸣qi₂ ⸢uzu⸣[ZA₃;LU] uzuME.ḪE₂ u ⸢uzu⸣[KA.IZI] ⸢tu-ṭaḫ⸣-ḫa KAŠ SAG ⸢GEŠTIN⸣ [x]

o 55

BALqi₂ ab-ru a-na ⸢de₂⸣-a u dASAR.LU₂.ḪI ⸢tu-nam⸣-[mar]

o 66

uduSISKUR₂ a-na de₂-a u dASAR.LU₂.ḪI BAL⸢qi₂⸣ [x x]

(o 6) You lay out an offering to Ea and Asalluhi. You libate [...], wine (and) milk. You sing the taqribtu-lament 'Come out like Utu ...' (and) the eršammakku-lament 'Woe, who destroyed'. In the morning on the roof of the temple of those gods you sprinkle pure water where you have swept.

o 77

GEŠTIN GA BALqi₂ d⸢utu⸣-gin₇ e₃-ta UB LA U₂? ⸢ER₂?⸣

o 88

u₃-ba mu-ḫul ER₂.ŠEM₄.MA ŠIR₃ ina še-ri₃ ina UR₃

o 99

E₂ DINGIR-MEŠ šu-a-tu a-šar ta-aš!(ŠI-)⸢biṭ!(KID) A KU₃-MEŠ ta-⸢sal-laḫ₃⸣

o 1010

3 GI.DU₈-MEŠ a-na de₂-a dUTU u dASAR.LU₂.ḪI ⸢KEŠ₂⸣

(o 10) You set out 3 offering tables for Ea, Šamaš and Asalluhi.

o 1111

3 GADA-MEŠ ina muḫ-⸢ḫi⸣ KI.⸢TUŠ⸣-MEŠ GARan NINDA.I₃.DE₂.AM₃ ⸢LAL₃⸣

(o 11) You place 3 linen cloths on the seats. You set in place a cake (made) of honey, butter, dates, fine flour (and) filtered oil. You set up [...] adagurru-containers (full) of first-class beer, wine (and) milk. [...] a censer (full) of juniper.

o 1212

I₃.NUN.NA ZU₂.LUM.MA zi₃EŠA I₃.GIŠ BARA₂.GA GARan [x]

o 1313

A.DA.GUR₅ KAŠ SAG GEŠTIN GA GUBan NIG₂.NA šimLI [x x]

o 14-1514-15

ŠE.NUMUN DU₃.A.BI DUBaq 3 uduSISKUR₂ BALqi₂ ⸢uzu⸣[ZA₃];⸢LU uzu⸣ME.ḪE₂ u uzu⸢KA⸣.IZI tu-ṭaḫ-ḫa KAŠ ⸢SAG⸣ [x x]

(o 14-15) You strew all (kinds of) seed. You lay out 3 offerings. You present shoulder, fatty tissue and roast meat. You libate first-class beer, [...]. You set up water. You draw the curtain. You sing opposite the temple 'He weeps [...] the just temple'. After this he sings to Ea, Šamaš [...], with the halhallatu-drum, 'Important one, return to me', 'Utu is king' (and) '... tears'. He stops. [...] the water [...].

o 1616

BALqi₂ A-MEŠ GUBan ⸢tug₂šid-du⸣ GID₂.DAad e₂ ⸢zid⸣ [...]

o 1717

er₂ im-še₈-še₈ ⸢ina⸣ [ter]-⸢ṣi⸣ E₂ ŠIR₃ ⸢EGIR⸣-[šu₂]

o 1818

dilmunki niŋin-na d⸢utu⸣ [lugal]-⸢am₃⸣ er₂ [...]

o 1919

ina ḫal-ḫal-la-at a-⸢na de₂⸣-[a d]⸢UTU⸣ [...]

o 2020

ŠIR₃ i-kal-la A-[MEŠ ...]

o 2121

ne₂-pe-ši ša₂ ŠU-MIN [...]

(o 21) The ritual procedures in the domain of the [...].
